title: casuarina distyla var. rigida
intro: Casuarina distyla var. rigida, also known as the New Zealand ironwood, is a variety of the Casuarina distyla species, a type of evergreen tree native to New Zealand and Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Casuarina distyla var. rigida include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide relief from various health issues.
